title: The 'Star Wars' Rankings and the 'Andor' Miracle
format: A May the 4th conversation about why Andor works and where the Star Wars films rank.

segments.heading: The 'Andor' Miracle
segments.note: why the show works, and its cinematic DNA

films.title: Michael Clayton
films.note: Tony Gilroy's, the acting-style benchmark the hosts keep citing: 'the Michael Clayton school of acting.'

films.title: Clerks
films.note: Kevin Smith's Randal-and-Dante bit about Death Star contractors, where Chris first saw the Empire thought of as labor and cost.

films.title: The Parallax View
films.note: The two-men-one-holds-the-power scene Sean compares to Krennic and Partagaz.

films.title: The Conversation
films.note: Hackman's surveillance dread as a model for the show's watchers.

films.title: How to Blow Up a Pipeline
films.note: Offered as a rare recent direct-action thriller.

films.title: One Battle After Another
films.note: Its cross-cut double-agent climax rhymes with the Ghorman massacre and put Battle of Algiers back in the conversation.

films.title: The Battle of Algiers
films.note: A clear influence: orchestrated uprising and state provocation.

films.title: Z
films.note: Costa-Gavras political thriller Chris names as source DNA.

films.title: State of Siege
films.note: The other Costa-Gavras touchstone.

films.title: Army of Shadows
films.note: Melville's French Resistance procedural.

films.title: Stalag 17
films.note: Prison-break lineage behind the Narkina 5 arc.

films.title: The Bridge on the River Kwai
films.note: Prison-camp epic in the same mold.

films.title: Le Trou
films.note: Jacques Becker's methodical escape film.

films.title: A Man Escaped
films.note: Bresson's escape film, another prison-break ancestor.

films.title: Where Eagles Dare
films.note: The WWII adventure-resistance strain.

films.title: Hangmen Also Die!
films.note: Fritz Lang's resistance drama.

films.title: The Guns of Navarone
films.note: The men-on-a-mission shape, shifting landscapes and all.

films.title: The Third Man
films.note: Post-war racketeering-and-espionage mood, Sean's favorite register.

films.title: The Conformist
films.note: Bertolucci; people operating on the fringes of a vulnerable society.

films.title: The Lives of Others
films.note: Surveillance-state paranoia in the same key.

films.title: Inglourious Basterds
films.note: Where Chris placed Richard Sammel, the Ghorman leader, from 'the bear Jew' scene.

films.title: Taxi Driver
films.note: From the '76 draft tangent on conspiracy-era cinema.

films.title: Network
films.note: Same tangent, the paranoid mid-'70s that never expired.

films.title: All the President's Men
films.note: Same tangent.

films.title: The Mandalorian and Grogu
films.note: Sean saw about a fifth of it and wasn't impressed, a big show that felt small on screen.

films.title: Star Wars: Starfighter
films.note: The Gosling vehicle; the hosts guess a lighter, more heroic tone.

segments.heading: The 'Star Wars' Rankings
segments.note: movies only, best first. Andor (No. 3) and The Mandalorian (No. 10) are TV, excluded below
segments.subject: Star Wars

films.title: The Empire Strikes Back
films.note: No. 1 overall. Sean's, by a coin flip Chris conceded: 'because it's your podcast.'

films.title: Star Wars
films.note: No. 2 overall. A New Hope; Chris is 'in an A New Hope phase' and rates it over Empire: 'it rocks from the first note to the last.'

films.title: Return of the Jedi
films.note: No. 4 overall, behind Andor. 'Underrated at this point in my life.'

films.title: Star Wars: The Last Jedi
films.note: No. 5 overall. Sean plays Switzerland on it: great sequences, a tedious Luke-and-Rey stretch, an awful casino planet.

films.title: Rogue One: A Star Wars Story
films.note: No. 6 overall. 'Barely a movie,' archetypes slung together, but the highs earn the meeting.

films.title: Star Wars: Episode III - Revenge of the Sith
films.note: No. 7 overall.

films.title: Star Wars: The Force Awakens
films.note: No. 8 overall. Han on the ship, Kylo killing his father: 'movie chills,' even as it mimics A New Hope.

films.title: Star Wars: Episode II - Attack of the Clones
films.note: No. 9 overall. 'Yoda's fighting Count Dooku. I'm just a kid again.'

films.title: Star Wars: Episode I - The Phantom Menace
films.note: No. 11 overall. Nearly unwatchable to Sean, but Chris overrules him for Duel of the Fates.

films.title: Solo: A Star Wars Story
films.note: No. 12 overall. Sean tried to revive it and couldn't: a movie made with no clear intention.

films.title: Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker
films.note: No. 13 overall. 'The worst thing that Star Wars has done,' and a consequential one.
